title: T-029 — Wiring: D365 push worker (WO confirmations) + DLQ retry
prompt:
# T-029 — Wiring: D365 push worker (WO confirmations) + DLQ retry

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §13.4, §13.5, §13.7, §13.10
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
D365 push worker for WO confirmations with retry + DLQ + idempotency.

Outbox event 'wo.confirmation' is produced by 08-PRODUCTION (not part of this task). Here build the consumer + retry mechanism. Idempotency same scheme as pull (T-028). Successful push flips d365_sync_jobs.status='completed'. KPI: ≥95% success rate target.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- lib/integrations/d365/push.ts [create]
- app/api/technical/d365/dlq/[id]/retry/route.ts [create]
- tests/wiring/d365-push.test.ts [create]

## Out of scope
- Pull (T-028)
- DLQ UI (T-058)

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given mocked D365 returns 500 three times, when worker runs with retry policy, then row inserted into d365_sync_dlq with error_message NOT NULL.
2. Given DLQ row, when retry endpoint called and mock returns 200, then DLQ.resolved_at set.
3. Given duplicate idempotency_key, when D365 returns 409, then job marked completed (already accepted).
4. Given user without technical.d365.sync.trigger, when retry endpoint called, then 403.

## Test strategy
- RED tests covering retry/backoff and DLQ semantics.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/wiring/d365-push.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not retry forever — cap at 3 then DLQ.
- Do not push when integration.d365.enabled=false.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
